summary: Tested ShipStation V2 shipping-rates integration + new shipping_cents override across backend (10 pytest tests, all passed) and frontend Checkout.jsx (live DC-destination rates render with 23 options across USPS/UPS/FedEx, cheapest auto-selected, summary updates). Discovered ONE CRITICAL backend bug: /checkout/square ignores payload.shipping_cents when computing the actual Square charge — so customers will still be charged the legacy $8 flat-rate even though the frontend forwards the chosen ShipStation rate.

critical.issue: square_router.py line 188 calls _calc_totals(subtotal) WITHOUT passing payload.shipping_cents. Result: order document's shipping_cents/total_cents and the Square charge amount always use the hardcoded STUFFIES_SHIPPING_CENTS ($8.00) regardless of which ShipStation rate the buyer selected. shipping_service / shipping_carrier / shipping_rate_id ARE stored on the order, but the dollars charged are wrong. Confirmation email totals are also wrong. This defeats the entire ShipStation feature. Fix: change line 188 to `tax_cents, shipping_cents, total_cents = _calc_totals(subtotal, payload.shipping_cents)`.

minor.issue: Empty-string city/state/postal_code passes Pydantic str validation; current 400 check works but consider Field(min_length=1) on RateAddress fields for cleaner schema-level rejection (422).

integration_issues.issue: When rates-error appears (e.g. ZIP 00000), the order summary still shows '$8.00 Shipping' / Total inflated by $8 because selectedShippingCents becomes null and the quote-cart call falls back to the legacy $8 default. Payment is correctly gated (user can't submit), but the summary is misleading. Recommendation: when there is a rates-error or no selectedRate, either hide the Shipping row, show '—', or pass shipping_cents:0 in the quote-cart payload until a rate is chosen.

issues: Signup-popup modal (Ride the wave with us) auto-opens on top of the checkout page after a few seconds and can intercept clicks on shipping-rate options. Consider suppressing the popup on /checkout (where the user has clear intent to convert).

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: square_router.py:188 — pass payload.shipping_cents into _calc_totals so the Square charge, order doc, and confirmation email use the actually-selected ShipStation rate.
Recompute totals only when a rate is actually selected so the summary doesn't show $8 fallback during a rates-error state.
Suppress the email-signup popup on the /checkout route to avoid blocking rate-radio clicks.
Optional: add min_length=1 constraints to RateAddress so missing city/postal yields a clean 422 from Pydantic instead of our manual 400.
critical_code_review_comments: square_router._calc_totals override path is implemented but never wired into the actual checkout_square endpoint — only quote-cart uses it. This is the core bug.
shipstation_router correctly excludes usps_media_mail + international USPS for US shipments, de-dups by (carrier, service), sorts cheapest-first. Looks production-quality.
shipstation_router exposes raw upstream error_messages in 400 detail — good for UX (e.g. 'Invalid destination postal code').

Checkout.jsx debounces rate fetch on every address field keystroke. For users typing each character, this fires many ShipStation calls before they're done. Consider debouncing 400-600ms or only fetching once postal is 5 digits.

success_rate.frontend: 85% — rendering & auto-select work; one summary-during-error display issue; programmatic rate-change in Playwright is a test-interaction quirk, not a UI bug

test_credentials: Admin: community@rollingriver.com / Camp1993! (not needed for these tests).
seed_data_creation: Cart seeded via localStorage key mr_cart_v1 with myrtle-stuffie (qty 1). No DB writes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_shipstation_checkout.py. After main agent fixes square_router.py:188 to pass payload.shipping_cents into _calc_totals, add an assertion that verifies an order doc created with shipping_cents=661 actually persists shipping_cents=661 (currently can't end-to-end test without a real Square nonce, but you can mock client.payments.create or just inspect the order in db.orders after a fake-source 400). Frontend cart key is mr_cart_v1 (not mr_cart). CartProvider only reads localStorage on mount, so seed BEFORE the first page load.
rca of the issue: The new shipping_cents override was correctly added to (1) the Pydantic CheckoutRequest model, (2) the order_doc persistence dict (shipping_service/carrier/rate_id), and (3) /checkout/quote-cart. However the actual /checkout/square endpoint's totals calculation at square_router.py:188 was not updated — it still calls _calc_totals(subtotal) with no second arg, so it always uses the SHIP_CENTS fallback. Reproduction: POST /api/checkout/square with shipping_cents=661 + a Stuffies item → resulting order doc / Square amount_money / email will show $8.00 shipping instead of $6.61. Mitigation: one-line fix — `tax_cents, shipping_cents, total_cents = _calc_totals(subtotal, payload.shipping_cents)`.
